title: Rushing Wind

sections.label: Lyrics
lines: In the silence we waited, hearts like dry ground
Eyes to the heavens, ears to the sound
Of a promise, once spoken, now breathed from the skies
The hush before thunder, the calm before rise
Then came the wind—not of storm, but of birth
A breath not from earth, but beyond all the earth
It filled every chamber, it rattled the stone
Lit fires on shoulders, made strangers feel known
Every tongue found a voice, every fear turned to flame
And the world heard the sound, but none heard it the same
**Like the sound of rushing wind, we rise again
Carried on the breath that woke the flame within
Hearts ignited, spirits wide
We are many, but one fire, one light inside
Like the sound of rushing wind, we won't be still—
Heaven in our lungs, and power in our will**
No wall could contain it, no border divide
The Spirit poured out like the turning of tides
From Jerusalem’s stones to the ends of the land
Love wrote its gospel in every hand
Speak, O breath, through shattered doubt
Rain down wisdom, sing it out
Flames not of ruin, but flames that restore
Burning the closed heart, opening doors
Every silence was shattered, every soul found its name
Every whisper grew louder, till none stayed the same
**Like the sound of rushing wind, we rise again
Carried on the breath that woke the flame within
Hearts ignited, spirits wide
We are many, but one fire, one light inside
Like the sound of rushing wind, we won't be still—
Heaven in our lungs, and power in our will**
We are the echo, the voice set free
The breath of heaven through history
And we burn with a purpose no darkness can dim
Still moved by the sound of that rushing wind

epigraph: And suddenly there came a sound from heaven as of a rushing mighty wind
